## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Add a `verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` function plus `LockVerifyStatus` enum and `LockVerifyEntry` struct to `core/src/registry.rs`, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New `pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing }` in registry.rs; New `pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status }` in registry.rs; New `pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` — Ok(None) when lock absent, Ok(Some(vec)) when parsed; Wasm resolved via `registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm` (same path as `resolve()`; covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY); NotFound error on wasm path → Missing; hash mismatch → Mismatch; equal hashes → Match; Reuses existing `sha256()` primitive so a lock written by `write_lock_for` verifies clean; Four unit tests: absent-lock (Ok(None)), Match after write_lock_for, Mismatch on tampered hash, Missing on nonexistent lang wasm; Add `pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus}` to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; No new files, no Cargo.toml changes, no lock-format changes — purely additive; Not material: additive read-only primitive, no CLI surface, no version bump required
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Assess the plan to add a read-only grove.lock wasm sha256 verify primitive (verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) to core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Verified against actual code: lock JSON shape {version,grammars:[{name,version,wasm}]} matches write_lock_for@412; plan's parse of doc[grammars]/entry[wasm]/name is correct.; AC#2 holds: wasm_sha256@106 == sha256(self.wasm) and verify_lock recomputes sha256 over the same file bytes, so a fresh lock verifies all-Match.; Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm equals resolve@219's idx.root.join(lang)/grammar.wasm in steady state (index root seeded from registry_root).; Missing/Mismatch/None signals map correctly to NotFound / hash-diff / absent-file; Ok(None) is a clean non-error signal (AC#3).; Change is purely additive (new public types+fn, no signature/lock-format change) — matches 'no version bump' operational impact.; Advisory: index() memoizes root via OnceLock while verify_lock re-evaluates registry_root(); keep the Match test registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process GROVE_REGISTRY mutation) to avoid divergence.; Advisory: decide malformed-entry handling (entry missing wasm/name) deliberately; avoid silent unwrap.; Advisory: verify_lock takes the lock file path (like locked_langs), not a project root — T07 must pass the resolved grove.lock path.; All five acceptance criteria addressed; no changes required before implementation.
Findings: See key_changes — all findings mirrored there for schema compliance.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Add verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> plus LockVerifyStatus enum and LockVerifyEntry struct to core/src/registry.rs, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: Added pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing } to registry.rs; Added pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status } to registry.rs; Added pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> — Ok(None) when lock absent; Wasm resolved via registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm (same path as resolve()); NotFound I/O error → Missing; hash equality → Match; hash mismatch → Mismatch; Other I/O errors propagated via anyhow with context; Added verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent unit test; Added verify_lock_matches_after_write_lock_for unit test (fresh lock verifies all Match); Added verify_lock_detects_tampered_hash unit test (mutated wasm field → Mismatch); Added verify_lock_detects_missing_wasm unit test (nonexistent lang → Missing); Added pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; All 165 unit tests pass; 4 new verify_lock tests all green
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Reviewed the additive verify_lock primitive plus LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry types in core/src/registry.rs and the lib.rs re-export against the approved plan.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: All 8 plan/AC items met: types, verify_lock signature, wasm path resolution, status mapping, hash-format consistency, curated re-export, purely additive.; Independently ran cargo test verify_lock: 4 passed, 0 failed (161 filtered = 165 total, matches claim).; cargo clippy -p grove-cst --release --locked: clean, zero warnings.; Ok(None) on absent lock; NotFound->Missing; hash-eq->Match; diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagated with anyhow context (no silent swallow).; Fresh lock verifies all-Match: verify_lock reuses sha256() and write_lock_for pins wasm_sha256() delegating to same primitive.; Malformed entries (missing name/wasm) are continue-skipped, not unwrapped — deliberate non-panicking choice per plan-review advisory.; Doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.; Tests authentic and meaningful (round-trip Match, tampered->Mismatch, nonexistent->Missing, absent->None).; Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated; fine for read-only advisory primitive.; Advisory (non-blocking): temp-test files share process id but use distinct name stems; no collision.
Findings: All 8 plan/AC items met and independently verified.; cargo test verify_lock: 4/4 pass; clippy clean.; Correct Match/Mismatch/Missing/None signal mapping; errors propagated not swallowed.; Additive-only: no signature, lock-format, or Cargo.toml changes.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validated that verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry are correctly implemented in core/src/registry.rs against all five acceptance criteria.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: AC#1 PASS: verify_lock returns Ok(None) for absent lock file; test verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent passes.; AC#2 PASS: All three status variants (Match/Mismatch/Missing) tested and passing; assertions are specific.; AC#3 PASS: verify_lock calls sha256(&bytes); write_lock_for uses wasm_sha256() delegating to same sha256 — round-trip Match test confirms.; AC#4 PASS: Exactly 4 unit tests covering absent-lock, Match, Mismatch, Missing — all 4 pass.; AC#5 PASS: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test 287 total 0 failed; files end with newline.; Public surface: pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} confirmed at lib.rs:70.; Error handling: NotFound->Missing, other I/O errors propagated via anyhow, malformed entries skipped.; Additive only: no existing signatures, lock format, or Cargo.toml changed. No regressions.

# Collate
## Iron Laws

- Collation is a read-and-rewrite of generated markdown. Do not mutate any JSON record under `.forge/store/`; the store is the source of truth and collation flows downstream from it.
- Read `.forge/personas/collator.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store reads via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.
- Do NOT call `set-summary` or `set-bug-summary` from within collation. The collator writes markdown views and a `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` disk file only. Calling `set-summary` mutates the JSON store and violates Iron Law 1 (the store is the source of truth; collation flows downstream from it, not into it). The orchestrator reads `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` directly — no store write is needed.

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md — NOTE: this file uses an intentionally modified
     Store-Write Verification variant: collation typically writes markdown views (not JSON records),
     so the preamble explains when `forge_store` calls apply. Canonical fragment is reference only. -->
## Store-Write Verification

Collation typically writes markdown views, not JSON records. If a remediation
step does call `forge_store` and the call exits non-zero or the `PreToolUse`
write-boundary hook blocks the call (exit 2):

1. Parse the structured error (names the offending field + schema file).
2. Correct the JSON to satisfy the schema.
3. Retry. Repeat up to 3 times.
4. After 3 failures, halt and escalate with original payload, corrected payload, and all error messages.

Never set `FORGE_SKIP_WRITE_VALIDATION=1` — operator-only emergency switch.

## Algorithm

```
1. Preferred: Run Plugin Tool
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/collate.cjs [SPRINT_ID]`
   - If tool succeeds, proceed to Finalize

2. Fallback: Manual Collation
   - Read `.forge/config.json` for prefix, paths, project description
   - Read all sprint/task/bug/event JSONs from `.forge/store/`
   - Generate MASTER_INDEX.md (sprint registry, task registry, bug registry)
   - Generate per-sprint TIMESHEET.md (from events)
   - Generate any other configured views

3. Rebuild context pack:
   - Rebuild the architecture context pack so orchestrators have a fresh summary
     after any KB updates (architecture docs may have changed during the sprint):
     ```
     ENGINEERING=$(node .forge/tools/manage-config.cjs get paths.engineering 2>/dev/null || echo engineering)
     node .forge/tools/build-context-pack.cjs \
       --arch-dir "$ENGINEERING/architecture" \
       --out-md .forge/cache/context-pack.md \
       --out-json .forge/cache/context-pack.json
     ```
   - On exit 1 (architecture directory absent), skip silently.

4. Finalize:
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.
   - Write `WRITEBACK-SUMMARY.json` to the sprint's artifact directory — use the sprint record's `path` field (read it from the store), not a reconstructed `engineering/sprints/{sprintId}/` template — with the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what views were regenerated>",
       "key_changes": ["<up to 6 bullets — which files were written>"],
       "verdict":     "n/a",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>"
     }
     ```
     The orchestrator reads this file directly to compose the collation event narrative. Do NOT call `set-summary` to register it — that would mutate the store in violation of Iron Law 1.
   - KB link refresh (best-effort; skip if unavailable):
     When running standalone in the Claude Code TUI, invoke via the Skill tool
     (`skill: "forge:refresh-kb-links"`).
     When running as a subagent within forge-cli (Pi runtime), the TS orchestrator
     calls `runRefreshKbLinks` directly after this phase — skip this step.
```

## Identity

I am the grove Collator. I deterministically regenerate markdown views from the JSON store. I do not make AI judgements — I invoke the generated tool or fall back to manual collation per spec.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):

- `MASTER_INDEX.md` — project-wide navigation hub

- `INDEX.md` — per-directory navigation hubs
- `COLLATION_STATE.json` — last collation metadata

Run the vendored collate tool:
```bash

the same outputs following the collation algorithm in

# Sprint: .grove/config.json declared mode + grove doctor health check

> Sprint ID: GROVE-S03
> Status: 🔵 active
> Execution Mode: sequential

## Goal

Make grove's integration mode a single declared fact in .grove/config.json (read identically by serve, agent steering, and init), converge every harness file on a mode switch via one reconcile_harness code path, and add a read-only grove doctor pre-flight health check that catches declared-mode-vs-harness drift and provider-down conditions and gates CI via its exit code.

## Description

Consolidates two coupled design docs (docs/adr/0002-grove-project-config-and-declared-mode.md + docs/doctor-command-proposal.md). ADR 0002 fixes the sticky/emergent-mode bug class (sticky explore surface, orphaned AGENTS.md) by introducing GroveConfig (.grove/config.json, version 1) with ExploreConfig demoted to an explore section, making serve read the declared mode, adding reconcile_harness as the single harness writer, a display-only mode badge in the config TUI, one-time legacy explore.json migration (deprecated immediately), and the explore.mode->steering rename. grove doctor (core::doctor typed Report + CLI verb) then adds harness-drift detection, explore provider probe, grove.lock sha256 verify, and --json/CI exit code, sequenced after the config work. Intake decisions: D1 both docs one sprint (doctor after config); D2 rename included; D3 all four doctor capabilities must-have; D4 legacy migration deprecated immediately (retained one-time auto-migrate + warning, not indefinite). Requirements: engineering/sprints/GROVE-S03/SPRINT_REQUIREMENTS.md.

## Summary of Collation Work

### 3. **Rebuilt Context Pack**

### 4. **Finalized Artifacts**
